description: Inputs that attempt to trigger circular reference handling. JSON.parse cannot produce circular references from a string (circular refs are a JavaScript object graph property, not a JSON text property), but some implementations have bugs around reference tracking. All inputs here are string-level anomalies that are shape violations.

inputs.label: repeated-open-brackets
inputs.rationale: Nested open brackets — first inner '[' is non-integer at position 1; SyntaxError

inputs.label: self-referential-notation
inputs.rationale: Nested array elements — second element '[2,[3]]' is not an integer; SyntaxError on '['
inputs.clarification: Tests that nested structure is refused, not just deeply nested

inputs.label: mismatched-brackets
inputs.rationale: Closing '}' instead of ']'; SyntaxError on '}' at closing position

inputs.label: double-close-bracket
inputs.rationale: Trailing ']' after valid close — eofCheck fires with trailing content

inputs.label: unclosed-bracket
inputs.rationale: No closing bracket — SyntaxError at EOF when expecting ']'

inputs.label: only-open-bracket
inputs.rationale: Single '[' with no content — EOF before ']' triggers SyntaxError

inputs.label: alternating-brackets
inputs.rationale: Three empty bracket pairs — first '[]' might parse as empty list, but trailing '[]' is caught by eofCheck

inputs.label: bracket-in-middle
inputs.rationale: Empty array element '[]' in integer list; SyntaxError on '[' at third element

inputs.label: reversed-brackets
inputs.rationale: Reversed brackets — ']' at position 0 is not '['; SyntaxError immediately

inputs.label: nested-empty-arrays
inputs.rationale: Array of empty arrays — first element '[]' triggers SyntaxError on inner '['

inputs.label: valid-empty-list-benign
inputs.rationale: Empty list is valid per spec — control input
